welcome.label: // vision-plugin
welcome.title:
Explore the Claude Code plugin
we want to ship.
welcome.subtitle: This workspace exposes a shareable plugin structure for Studio Kontrast projects: .claude-plugin/plugin.json, review agents, domain skills, hooks, MCP configuration, and the docs needed to install it in product repos.
welcome.secondary: Switch back to my-project any time to compare the generic Claude Code concepts with a production-oriented plugin.

plugins.title: Plugin Manifest
plugins.description: How the plugin is named, versioned, and distributed across projects.

agents.title: Review Agents
agents.description: Subagents for PR orchestration, code review, security, performance, testing, and architecture.

skills.title: Domain Skills
skills.description: Reusable knowledge packs for Next.js frontend, backend, API, security, testing, deployment, and database work.

hooks.title: Hooks & Guards
hooks.description: Automation that protects branch policy and keeps plugin changes disciplined.

mcp.title: MCP Integrations
mcp.description: External context sources such as Zeplin that support design engineering work.

docs.title: Project Docs
docs.description: README, changelog, and licensing notes that explain installation and rollout.

children.name: plugin.json
children.label: Plugin Manifest
children.description: The identity card for the plugin: namespace, version, metadata, and distribution intent.

children.name: pr-orchestrator.md
children.label: PR Orchestrator
children.description: Lead reviewer that classifies a diff and delegates to specialist agents.

children.name: code-reviewer.md
children.label: Code Reviewer
children.description: Checks bugs, architecture fit, conventions, and risky implementation details.

children.name: security-reviewer.md
children.label: Security Reviewer
children.description: Focuses on RBAC, secrets, uploads, webhooks, and API route hardening.

children.name: performance-reviewer.md
children.label: Performance Reviewer
children.description: Looks for bundle regressions, data-loading waste, and expensive rendering patterns.

children.name: test-coverage.md
children.label: Test Coverage Reviewer
children.description: Flags missing unit, integration, and E2E coverage in changed areas.

children.name: architect.md
children.label: Architect
children.description: Helps design PIM, DAM, ERP, and e-commerce application architecture with explicit trade-offs.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: Frontend Patterns
children.description: Patterns for Next.js admin interfaces, tables, dashboards, forms, and performance.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: Backend Patterns
children.description: Guidance for services, repositories, auth, queues, and structured logging.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: Security Review
children.description: OWASP-style checks adapted for RBAC-heavy business applications.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: E2E Testing
children.description: Playwright guidance for multi-step workflows, RBAC, and import or export flows.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: Deployment Patterns
children.description: Next.js deployment rules for Docker, CI, health checks, and rollback.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: API Design
children.description: Resource naming, pagination, filtering, versioning, and error response conventions.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: Database Patterns
children.description: Safe migrations, indexing, RLS, and query design for data-heavy catalogs.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: Git Workflow
children.description: Branch naming, protected branch policy, and PR rules for the plugin rollout.

children.name: hooks.json
children.label: Hook Configuration
children.description: Plugin-scoped hook definitions that protect git pushes and remind contributors to keep docs aligned.

tree.name: .mcp.json
tree.label: MCP Configuration
tree.description: Project-scoped external context, starting with Zeplin for design specs.

children.name: branch-guard.sh
children.label: Branch Guard Script
children.description: Shell guard that denies dangerous `git push` commands from protected branches.

tree.name: README.md
tree.label: README
tree.description: Overview, installation instructions, and rollout advice for the plugin.

tree.name: CHANGELOG.md
tree.label: Changelog
tree.description: Initial release notes for the plugin scaffold.

tree.name: LICENSE
tree.label: License
tree.description: Distribution status for the plugin while it remains internal.

terminal.welcomeTagline: Inspect the plugin manifest, then drill into the review agents and domain skills that make it reusable.

quickStart.description: see the project defaults captured for the team

howToExplore: Start with the plugin manifest to see the namespace and metadata
Open the review agents and follow their related files into skills
Finish in the README to see installation and rollout guidance

doctorChecks.label: Hooks
doctorChecks.detail: branch guard ready for git push checks

initConventions: PRs target staging, never production directly
memoryEntries: Backoffice apps use RBAC with admin, manager, and contributor roles
Review verdicts escalate only when confidence is above 80%
Protected branches are staging and production
Zeplin remains the primary design context source
Database guidance must assume large product catalogs and order history
